title: Such a disappointment
review_excerpt: We checked into the resort with high hopes of having a luxury holiday for our family. Such a beautiful and picturesque place. We arrived with our two children 2 and 4 booking a 2 bedroom villa. Unfortunately we weren't able to use our second room as it was in a completely sepa...
review_text: We checked into the resort with high hopes of having a luxury holiday for our family. Such a beautiful and picturesque place. We arrived with our two children 2 and 4 booking a 2 bedroom villa. Unfortunately we weren't able to use our second room as it was in a completely separate structure separated by the plunge pool. All three rooms (2 bedrooms and living area) had subpar cleaning with cobwebs throughout the rooms. We actually requested the cobwebs cleaned in the living space since with was throughout the high ceiling area and was rather unsightly. Both bathtubs had staining and mold around the drains which were cleaned properly when requested. Definitely not what you would expect from a $1800 USD a night resort. The food selection was limited. There was one pool with limited service available there. The beach, while beautiful, was a hike to get to and once you did had a beach that was not swimmable. It was actually quite dangerous. We planned a 3 night stay but left after the second night. When we checked out no one ever asked how our stay was or apologized for the cleanliness. When I asked if there were accomadations that were continuous so we could be in the same structure as the kids but still have a separate room we were told to get a sitter to stay with them in the other room so they could be on their own. We were sorely disappointed and would not recommend this resort to anyone - family or couple.

mismatch_types: low aspect rating with positive text sentiment
title: Bad Villa Condition and Sloppy Housekeeping
review_excerpt: This resort supposedly represents the pinnacle of luxury in Bali as well as in Asia and have spectacular views. Indeed this hotel / resort is unlike any I have stayed before. The Bulgari chain only have two hotels in the world, and strive to deliver cutting edge luxury with co...
review_text: This resort supposedly represents the pinnacle of luxury in Bali as well as in Asia and have spectacular views. Indeed this hotel / resort is unlike any I have stayed before. The Bulgari chain only have two hotels in the world, and strive to deliver cutting edge luxury with contemporary design and rely on the drawing power of the Bvlgari brand. One thing you need to realize first is that this resort is very very expensive, the most pricey in Bali with rack rates starting in the range of thousand dollars per night. Booked the upper tier cliff ocean villa which has a direct ocean front view. However, it may not make sense to pay for the extras since you can get the same or better view from the resort’s common areas. The villa is divided neatly into outdoor and indoor area. The outdoor area comprises the dinning area, living room, a small ( and I mean very small ) garden and a very small plunge pool ( for dipping only, not for swimming ). The indoor area comprises of the bedroom, closet and bathroom. As expected, the design is modern contemporary and there’s a lot of attention to detail using much custom furniture and fittings. There’s also a stylish B&O TV which I enjoyed very much playing music and watch movies in it. . Floor to Ceiling wood panel windows allow the ocean view to pour into the room. Bathroom is a full ensuite with a deep soaking tub and is nicely designed but a bit too dark ( black is the color used here ). Nice Bvlgari toiletries provided. Bed is very comfy and sheets and bed linens are all first class. It’s also kind of nice to fall asleep listening to the ocean sounds. Unfortunately, there are also too many negatives. Things are not that easy to figure out, starting with the annoying doors that you have to twist the knob and key to a certain position otherwise it won’t open. There are many switches in the villa which took me quite a while to figure out, and I am used to high-tech hotel rooms. The room is also very dark and dim, especially at night with the predominant black and dark brown color scheme. Wireless Internet is complimentary but slow and unreliable. The worse of all is the wear and tear evident everywhere throughout the villa ( see photos ) and this resort is just barely over two years old, the result of using wood extensively which bears the scratch marks over prolong use and is rather unsightly. One standing lamp is so severely stained that it looked like someone just vomited over it. Strange that for the price this resort is charging, it is not doing more to in refurbishing and maintenance and it shows. I have stayed at older hotels and resorts which much better condition. It's a matter of whether the hotel is willing to invest in refurbishment. The wear and tear problem is exacerbated by the sloppy housekeeping of this resort. Dirt and stains all over the villa, cups not cleaned properly and strands of human hair in one of the amenity boxes ( see photos ). Totally unacceptable. Finally, to round up the series of annoying incidents, the aircon became warm middle of the night ( I have to adjust the temperature down to compensate ) and for some weird reasons, the cordless phone gave out a peeping sound too. I did complain about the condition of the villa and the sloppy housekeeping. The resort then waived my F&B charges and promised a complimentary night stay which I shall see if they will fulfilled, more as an evidence that the management is interested to rectify the problem rather than I want to come back to try this resort. Disappointingly, the General Manager whom I was told was informed of this complaint, did not bother to personally apologize to me and instead just sent one of his staff to discuss with me about their proposed compensation. He did sent a email afterwards though. The common areas are very nice, with a nice infinity pool ( but again small ), two restaurants, a bar and a gift shop and even a Bvlgari boutique right inside the resort. There’s a beach but can’t swim in it because of high tide. As expected, the prices for everything here is very expensive. Landscaping throughout the resort is well done indeed and it’s a pleasure to walk around to explore. Service is passable without any major fault but nothing very outstanding as well. Typical of Bali, they will take some time to attend to your request, it’s not immediate. But then this is not a business hotel so perhaps that is to be tolerated. One word of caution. The entire hotel seems to revolve around a specialized group of clientele only, ie the honeymooners and couples looking for a romantic getaway. So only come here to stay if this is your sole purpose, otherwise you will feel seriously out of place and there’s nothing much to do since this place is so secluded. Even if this is your purpose, for the price, there must be better value elsewhere in Bali which boasts literally hundreds of luxury resorts and villas. So in conclusion, this resort is way below expectation. I got a villa in poor condition with extremely sloppy housekeeping and overall, I have to rate this resort a low 2/5. Not withstanding the compensation offered by the resort, all these problems should not have happened in the first place. It’s such a pity because this is frankly a very beautiful resort and if maintain and run properly, has all the potential for greatness.

title: Disappointed, doesn't match up to the price tag
review_excerpt: With this price tag, one would expect great linens, clean and comfortable. On our first day, we decided to take it easy and had an afternoon nap. To our greatest shock, the bed had sand all over... Got them to change the sheets. But bed linens were rough. Totally not what I wo...
review_text: With this price tag, one would expect great linens, clean and comfortable. On our first day, we decided to take it easy and had an afternoon nap. To our greatest shock, the bed had sand all over... Got them to change the sheets. But bed linens were rough. Totally not what I would expect with this price tag. On our second day, while we left our villa for the spa which was just seconds walk from our villa, for a mere 2 hours, the monkeys decided to invite themselves in for a feast, despite the fact that all the doors were shut. My iPad and my husband's cameras had "paw" prints and fruit juices all over. His glasses case had teeth marks... Spa - I would give a 5* for the spa treatment received - both my husband and I had very good therapist. One feedback - please change the in-spa slippers - very uncomfortable to be wearing a pair of room slippers outdoor enroute to the spas on grass and tiles. Especially when it's raining, the room slippers with very thin sole gets wet and it's very uncomfortable to walk with on hard and uneven grounds... Apart from that - congratulations - your spa deserves a 4* for overall experience. F&B - Only had 2 restaurants to choose from and 1 other one by the supposed beach which only serves lunch. In the blistering hot and humid days, it's impossible to even consider dining by the beach... The 2 restaurants - 1 serves local food and 1 Italian. To be honest, we didn't try the Italian - thus, no comments. The local - disappointing. Selection of local food was not great, some items in the menu may even be a tad too fancy. While people who visit the resort are generally fairly well-to-do, it's simply unnecessary to have fancy stuff like lobster and wagyu to make a simple fried rice or noodle, not unless you can do it really well. Balinese sushi in the menu - interesting twist but the seaweed used was not fresh. Tasted like those I have at home which has past the sell-by date. Breakfast was ultra disappointing. A hotel/resort with such price tags - served fruit juices from the bottle - just like those you would get from any 3/4 star ones - worst of all, no labeling - four types of juices served - orange, mango, pineapple and apple - and in a not so brightly-lit place, it was quite a challenge to identify. Chinese congee was lumpy while the little dish of curry chicken was fairly tasty. Although I've asked for late check-out, I don't think I wish to stay in this place much longer. I got up at 5am due to the noise on the doors caused by strong wind but I later got worried wondering when the monkeys will come visit again... The hotel staff are generally quite friendly and nice but I've met 1 or 2 who couldn't quite understand english - 1 example - when I called to ask to transferred to a concierge, to help with restaurant recommendations on the phone, she told me that she didn't understand but if I wanted, she would make reservations for me...

mismatch_types: low aspect rating with positive text sentiment
title: pretentious and disappointing
review_excerpt: Me and my boyfriend spent 9 nights in Bulgari resort in Bali, and we were very excited to try out the new resort everyone was talking about. My first impression on bulgari was not good. Online reservation system was not giving much hope, so I left them a message to contact me....
review_text: Me and my boyfriend spent 9 nights in Bulgari resort in Bali, and we were very excited to try out the new resort everyone was talking about. My first impression on bulgari was not good. Online reservation system was not giving much hope, so I left them a message to contact me. Of course, nobody did, so I called their UK number. The girl on the phone couldn’t tell me the difference between differently priced rooms, told me my preferences were noted but not guaranteed, and managed to write down both name and e-mail wrong, even after several rounds of spelling (i found that out after not getting the confirmation e-mail, so called to see what’s going on). Second big disappointment came with an e-mail, asking if we’d like a pick-up at the airport, quoting several different prices for different types of vehicles. I must say it never ever happened to me before that a $1000+ per night resort charges for airport transfer. And what a charge this was! A half an hour ride from airport to the hotel goes from $85 to $100+, depending on the car you choose, in an island where a hotel car with a driver usually costs no more than $20 per hour. One more thing they also offered is immigration assistance, of course, coming again with a fee (must I say that every good hotel in Bali has this service included). My first impression on the resort was – none. We arrived in the evening, and the resort is so badly lit you can’t see anything of it really (need to be careful when walking to your room after that extra glass of wine for dinner). Same goes for the room – we were very disappointed with the design – both aesthetically and practically . Maybe we expected a lot more (being fans of both Antonio Citterio and Bulgari hotel in Milan), so our disappointment was bigger. The overall design is quite simple. The layout of the room is not very well thought off, all the doors are extremely narrow - we’re both quite slim, and yet we were banging our shoulders every time when going through the doors; the entrance to the room is blocked by a writing desk – if one is sitting at the desk, the other person going in/out needs to squeeze in order to go through; a corridor (containing wardrobes) with extremely low ceiling divides the bedroom and the bathroom, which are both quite big. The narrowness of the corridors and the overall feeling of claustrophobia is accentuated by noticeable lack of light, both sunlight and artificial lighting. The villa is gloomy both during the day and night - I’ve had mild headaches from huge effort to see better. Even the villa pool deck is in shade most of the day, so we kept chasing that little sun beam whenever we chose to stay in our room instead of going down to the pool. The welcome note left in the room addressed my boyfriend only, there wasn’t even a slightest hint on the note that they’re aware somebody’s accompanying him, even though I booked the room, and left them both our names and details. This manner was unfortunately utilized throughout our stay so that majority of staff was addressing him and him only – Good morning, sir, goodbye, sir, have a lovely day mr. I – when i’m obviously walking next to him. Wireless internet is available free of charge throughout the resort, and considering you’re in Bali, it’s not even that bad. There’s a TV with a DVD player in the bedroom, and a cd player with radio tuner in the bathroom. In-villa mini-bar offers a choice of sodas and beers, limited choice of fruit juices, no milk (on my first day I asked having milk in the fridge, so they brought some and refilled every day), biscuits only for snack, champagne (no wine – you need to call room service for that), some bad coffee and tea that tastes like weeds (having tried it the first day, I asked to have green tea instead, so they brought me jasmine green tea every day). The resort has two restaurants, italian and asian fusion. We were told by some people that the food is a bit “over the top”, and that’s exactly the right way to describe it. Especially in the italian restaurant – everything was served in such fashion, that I think it would scare even the most formal dinners–black tie-crowd. And yet, with no background for it – we were so disappointed with the food – almost everything on the menu was merely tasteless. Add to this minuscule portions, and you’re bound to stay hungry throughout your stay. With a healthy appetite, you should prepare yourself for the full , 5-course dinner in order to fill your tummy (or go out for dinners). I must say though that the grill @ the beach club is superb. But my biggest disappointment of all was coffee. At least I expected good coffee in a resort with an italian brand behind it. Nope. It was so bad we haven’t finished the cup once. We even gave them instructions on how to make it, after a series of bad lattes and cappuccinos, but the result was still disastrous. So we just gave up and drank tea instead, waiting for some better coffee days to some. Also, don’t expect enjoying your sunset mojito by the pool – we switched to corona after several unsuccessful cocktails. If you’re beach lovers, this resort is definitely not for you. The hotel is positioned high on a cliff, and it takes some stairs, an elevator, than stairs again to get to the beach, which is btw coral beach, so swimming is prohibited. A few more issues: they haven’t found a way to stop humidity from going into pillows and matress, so they were very smelly – i had the impression i could sense the smell of various people sleeping in that bed before me. The resort is crowded with villas – 59 – and you have no feeling of privacy. You constantly hear your neighbours talking and buggies going by. The pool and the pool are are to small for hotel this size – considering the pool in the villa is more like a bathtub than a pool, and that nobody goes to the beach – so it can be a bit crowded. Oh, and they haven’t adopted the wonderful no-signing policy, so you need to spare the extra few moments after every drink/meal to sign the bill. Also, everything is ridiculously expensive. After all these things, I must say the service was almost impeccable. The staff were always friendly and smiling, almost all of them spoke very good english and we almost never called for anything twice. Though I must say their friendliness sometimes bordered with annoying – it can be a bit tiring saying hello and goodbye 10 times/minute. We were often interrupted during our meal - at one occasion our dinner was interrupted 4 times in less than10 minutes by four different staff members, coming to ask if everything was alright. And then, there’s also the “addressing the mr only” thing. I need to say that some details are charming – a kite, a deck of cards and a little black book in your beach bag, great choice of bathroom amenities, fresh fruits and lovely desserts delivered daily and cute little presents left in your room leaves a nice touch. The goodbye present was very nice and original My intention was not to trash the hotel – it’s not a bad hotel, after all, but since it is trying to position itself to the level of aman resorts and it’s probably the most expensive hotel in bali, I feel I needed to comment on it exactly on that level. For this money (rooms start at $1060++) you’d be much better off in one of the amans or alternatively four seasons.

title: OK. BUT DECEPTIVELY ADVERTISED.
review_excerpt: At first glance, our villa was pretty, if awkwardly-laid-out. But upon closer examination, there were many shortcomings. Door-knobs fell off in my hand, the toilet flooded in the middle of the night, and we found 10 toenail clippings scattered around the main living area. When...
review_text: At first glance, our villa was pretty, if awkwardly-laid-out. But upon closer examination, there were many shortcomings. Door-knobs fell off in my hand, the toilet flooded in the middle of the night, and we found 10 toenail clippings scattered around the main living area. When we pulled back the sheets on one of our beds, we observed a number of tiny bugs. To top it off, the beach was closed. We felt that the website was misleading and made it look like guests could relax and swim on the beach. You can never swim on the beach, and on the day we were there, we were even unable to walk on the beach. There also appears to be a serious fly problem. Flies were swarming around us in our villa and at the restaurant. On the plus side, the staff was extremely pleasant and tried to be accommodating. Every employee we encountered was professional and dedicated. The person who welcomed us, and the butler who was assigned to our villa were amazing, and deserve to work at a facility that reflects their hard work. The property manager was extremely professional. The views were stunning, and the staff was very hospitable. Ultimately, however, we paid for a 5-star experience and got a 3-star result.

title: Only stayed for 18 hours - bugs and no running water!
review_excerpt: To be fair it was not one single person's fault that our stay at the Bulgari went horribly wrong. The employees are very well intentioned but they have not been trained as well as say Four Seasons staff. For $1,000 a night we expected perfection. We had already stayed at the F...
review_text: To be fair it was not one single person's fault that our stay at the Bulgari went horribly wrong. The employees are very well intentioned but they have not been trained as well as say Four Seasons staff. For $1,000 a night we expected perfection. We had already stayed at the Four Seasons Jimbaran Bay and Sayan which were fabulous. The Bulgari is very isolated down near Uluwatu. You cannot walk to town or anywhere outside the hotel and I don't believe there are many restaurants nearby so you are pretty stuck. The views are amazing...cliff-top overlooking the ocean. Rooms were very fancy but not Balinese at all. Huge bathroom and the outdoor space, including plunge pool were great. Now to the bad parts...when we arrived we went down the funicular to the ocean front cafe thinking we would have a casual lunch. We soon realized that the hotel does not do anything casual. It is literally a thatched hut with wooden tables but the only entrees were $45 steaks and seafood dishes. No sandwiches or salads to speak of. Horrible service and the food was inedible. We had dinner at the italian restaurant...food was ok, better service, very expensive. Back to our room where things went downhill...in the middle of the night my husband and I realized we had no running water. Called the front desk and they said they had no idea how long it would be to fix. Then we woke up at 6am and I turned on the light next to the bed and suddenly there were hundreds of bugs all over the bed. I thought at first they had been IN the bed and freaked out. I would post pictures of it here but it is too gross! Our butler came and said he had no idea what it was so we decided to pack up and leave the hotel (later found out it was from the villa roofing which they are supposed to treat for insects). We still had NO running water at 7am when we left and went back to the Four Seasons Jimbaran Bay. To their credit we did get our money back for the one night we stayed there and a gift and apology note were sent to us at the Four Seasons from the Bulgari. We heard that over the next several days they still were having water problems which apparently is not uncommon for them. They looked to be adding some Bulgari Villas but I think they need to get the water and bug situation under control before adding more rooms and guests!

title: Not befitting Bulgari Name, Overpriced Given The Drawbacks
review_excerpt: I was there for my honeymoon. Paid USD$1k per night. The location was very remote. Which is ok for a honeymoon and the views from the villa were spectacular and dramatic when we reached the hotel for the first time. However, this initial excitement wore thin when we encountere...
review_text: I was there for my honeymoon. Paid USD$1k per night. The location was very remote. Which is ok for a honeymoon and the views from the villa were spectacular and dramatic when we reached the hotel for the first time. However, this initial excitement wore thin when we encountered a number of issues with the hotel room. There were several power outages at the hotel. The electricity went off frequently. Also, whilst the villa had a pool, the pool was not clean. There was clumps of dust on the floor of the pool. Very bad. Spa was good but did not use Bulgari products. They did not replenish the bulgari frangrance at all. All we got was one 5 ml sample! Apart from the measly toileteries, the only link to Bulgari was an overpriced boutique store. Service was ok and the italian restaurant was good but generally very disappointing for USD$1k per night. SHould have stayed at the Four Seasons. There does not appear to be any proper management in this hotel. They did not even make any special arrangement such as a bottle of wine to make up for any of the glitches and the fact that we had indicated that this is a honeymoon for us.

title: Over rated !
review_excerpt: The scenery is really fantastic. But that is just it. We were there for my cousin wedding. We booked weeks ahead and requested a twin bed, but upon check in, it is not available. They were kind enough to offer extra bed in the room. But looking at the bed they brought in, the ...
review_text: The scenery is really fantastic. But that is just it. We were there for my cousin wedding. We booked weeks ahead and requested a twin bed, but upon check in, it is not available. They were kind enough to offer extra bed in the room. But looking at the bed they brought in, the bed must have been older than the hotel itself (Should have take photo of the extra bed). Dirty, holes, and with springs that can give you back pain. The room is in great need of renovation. The wooden floor creaks when you walk, some of the wood is rotten and maybe is due to the rainy season, so the wood kind of expand and you need to give the door an extra kick to open it. The worst is at night, i must have killed over twenty mosquito and yet they still keep coming. In the morning, was awaken by monkeys jumping on the roof of the villas. It's something new i must say. Around and in the hotel, there is pretty much nothing to do. One final word, if you are not here on honeymoon, better find another hotel.

title: Spent two nights being dripped on by leaking AC, potholes in floor of 'luxury' villa - stay at Ayana instead!
review_excerpt: I have honestly never been so disappointed by a five star hotel in my life. We left the Ayana (gorgeous, amazing service) and checked in to Bulgari to get married (I fell in love with the idea of a water chapel wedding and photos on the beach). I've seen all the reviews that t...
review_text: I have honestly never been so disappointed by a five star hotel in my life. We left the Ayana (gorgeous, amazing service) and checked in to Bulgari to get married (I fell in love with the idea of a water chapel wedding and photos on the beach). I've seen all the reviews that talk about how luxurious Bulgari is, and maybe I am just spoiled, but Bulgari cannot compare to any other 5 star villa resort that I have stayed at in Asia. Even in Uluwatu there are much nicer places with far better service that you can stay at for less spend. No one with any impact at the Bulgari resort seems to care whether you are satisfied or not. The guys that ferry you about by buggy, the waiters, even the chefs are so sweet but the management is just so nonchalant. The arrogance of the place is insane. 1. I am two months pregnant, and yet when we checked in to get married there was no one to meet us to check us in to the hotel we were getting married at! On my wedding day I had to haul suitcases and my wedding dress out of the taxi and actually go wandering around to look for where to check-in. Sorry, but even if you go to a 5-star hotel for dinner there's always a bell boy there as standard to welcome and help with any bags?! 2. First impressions of the villa - the varnish on the outdoor floor boards is peeling and faded. It just looks tired. It costs nothing to sand and revarnish floor boards?! I would never let my home floor boards get to this kind of state - and Bulgari is meant to be in the business of hospitality and luxury... 3. Speaking of floor boards, getting ready for my wedding, I fell down a small pot hole in the floor of the 'luxury' villa by the heel of my shoe (pictured). It might sound petty - but this is meant to be five star and the place is just worn out. There were black stains on our curtains, too. It was just depressing! 4. My biggest issue was the AC and the lack of concern of anyone in the hotel to fix it. On my WEDDING NIGHT I didn't get a wink of sleep (and not in the good way either!) because the AC dripped all over me - not in a little way - look at the photo of filthy brown water dripping on to the bed!! In the morning I complained to house keeping and the butler, who promised that an engineer would come and fix it. We returned to the room at around 6pm and it was still leaking, the duvet was soaked through. I called to complain again about the leak and the soaked bedsheets, and when we came back to the room a few hours later the sheets had been changed byt the AC was still faulty and dripping all over the new sheets - they hadn't done a thing to fix the AC. Beggars belief, I know. I complained again - begging someone to fix the AC so that I could get some sleep. The engineer's idea of 'fixing' the AC was to place a board over the vent. Sure, I wasn't getting dripped on from the bed anymore, but the bedroom was then sweltering hot - literally no AC (not even a basic fan out of courtesy) in a so-called luxury hotel for the whole night?!!! I'm pretty sure that in a tropical climate, that there is no hotel - of any star - that would do this to guests. Considering that this was meant to be the wedding of my dreams, it's almost as if Bulgari did everything that they could to make it miserable. To make matters worse the water that would have dripped on me re-routed to the left hand corner of the room - all over the beautiful jewelery my new husband had gifted me on my wedding day. It was just disgraceful. 4. The breakfasts were nice, and I will say that the coffee is good. Note that if you stay in Villa 25 you will need the caffeine to get you through the days after those humid, uncomfortable, sleepless nights. 5. The events coordinator is a vile woman who is more unhelpful than you can imagine. I only wanted to get married in Bali for the water chapel wedding and the photos on the beach. The beach was closed - had been for weeks, with no plans to open anytime soon. But no one thought to tell us this when we were paying the upfront, pretty hefty fee for the ceremony. My wedding planner hadn't been informed either. I only found out when I was asking the photographer on the day to take us to the beach for some nice shots on the sand. Instead of offering helpful alternative beach locations, the Bulgari lady just adamantly insisted that it wouldn't be possible. I was reduced to tears by this woman an hour before my ceremony. Luckily the photographer was a very kind Balinese man who took us to a public beach the following day to take some nice photos. 6. Heavy rain fall meant that my water chapel wedding - the only reason I wanted to get married at Bulgari - didn't happen. I'm not blaming Bulgari for the rain fall, and the team did do a lovely job of making the alternate venue, a gazebo, as nice as they could. The team comped a spa treatment for us because of the rain, which was nice. But Ayana (where we had stayed for the five days leading up to the wedding) also comped us a couple's massage just as a celebratory gesture to get married. I'm not being ungrateful - but comping spa visits is pretty standard when you are spending the kind of money that these resorts charge, especially when you are getting married, anniversaries, honeymoons, etc. Rant over. You won't catch me staying at Bulgari ever again, and we are regular visitors to Bali, especially Uluwatu. The grounds are very pretty and well maintained, but the villas are worn out and can't compete with the other luxury resorts in Uluwatu. Plus, the commitment to guest satisfaction is bordering zero. Stay at your own peril, people!

title: Probably one of the top 5 in Bali, but definitely not the best
review_excerpt: Before starting this review, I'd like to say that I have been to Bali in July/August 8 times already, in good conditions, and that I have been to many high end hotels and resorts on the island. I had heard many positive comments/reviews concerning the Bulgari and I was rather ...
review_text: Before starting this review, I'd like to say that I have been to Bali in July/August 8 times already, in good conditions, and that I have been to many high end hotels and resorts on the island. I had heard many positive comments/reviews concerning the Bulgari and I was rather excited to stay in this hotel for 4 nights. However I left with a contrasted impression due to several points I will now develop. Pros: -The situation is pretty decent, as the view is definitely breathtaking (one of the nicest in Bali), and the hotel is located less than half an hour away from the International Airport. -The hotel itself is design and, as it is only 34 months old, it is still in good condition. The decoration is modern and more tasteful than at the Four Seasons at Jimbaran Bay. -There are two very nice boutiques located within the hotel (they are pretty expensive though). -The breakfast, which is a hybrid between "all you can eat" buffet and a la carte, is delicious and offers a sumptuous view. -The bathroom is beautiful, spacious and clean. It contains 2 showers (outdoor and indoor) and a large bathtub, plus two sinks. Cons: -The service is OK, but definitely not worth the price. Service at Four Seasons Sayan/JImbaran Bay, Alila, any of the Aman hotels is clearly better, personnel is more attentive. A few examples: the buggy (which is indispensable to any movement in the hotel) once took 20 minutes to arrive at my room while "5 to 7 minutes" is announced; the staff at the restaurants is not as professional as in Mozaic, Cire or the restaurant of the Amandari for instance; we had booking issues at the restaurant because our butler forgot to make the reservation for us... -The food at the Italian restaurant, which is supposed to be the best of its kind on the island, is overpriced and not that great. -The beach is nice but it is almost impossible to go swim: the ground is full of rocks and the waves are more of the "surfing caliber" - high and dangerous. -The 2 rooms I've been into were infected with armies of tiny ants. I found some in my bag, inside my laptop... Some came under my keyboard (annoying), and BEHIND my screen (very annoying). In total, I probably killed around 40 before giving up and asking for some bug spray. -The cliffside villas have a great view but the ones that are located higher offer a pretty mediocre view. -The rooms are poorly lit. Huge windows compensate during the day, but the room is somber after sunset. To conclude, overall Bulgari is a classy and sumptuous 5 star hotel. However I would not recommend it to a friend as some other comparable hotels that offer relatively similar prestations are slightly better... and cheaper. I am thinking about the brand new Alila which is extremely design (almost making Bulgari look traditional), offers a better service and serves tastier food for a couple hundred dollars less per night. I would also recommend the Four Seasons at Jimbaran Bay, which is very similar to Bulgari (although older) but offers a truly professional service at a lower price.

review_text: I didn’t like my stay at the hotel. I will start with the cons. 1. Unnecessarily expansive. I paid over $1,000 a night for the room. Nothing in the hotel made me believe the hotel was worth a $1,000 a night. 2. Cleanliness. For what I paid I expected there not to be any bugs in my bed. One night we grabbed 6 ants and three spiders from the bed! And yes the door/window were always closed. Plus the entire villa had one trash can! 3. Service. Depends on who you were dealing with. I appreciate why hotels would have non-locals working at the establishment, in this case they had Japanese and Chinese staff members to deal with their Japanese and Chinese clients. But they should at least be able to speak English! I was also very disappointed by the Chinese lady at the concierge desk. When I asked her of good restaurants outside the hotel for dinner, without even making eye contact she handed me a little booklet of different places to visit from her top drawer! 4. Beach didn’t serve any beverages. Pros 1. The hotel probably has the best view in the world. No kidding the view from the top of the cliff can’t be described. 2. Food was great, and I really appreciated the fact that the included breakfast meant I can order it from a menu in my room. First time. 3. Monkeys. They came to our villa, a dozen of them, it was the most exciting thing about this trip. The hotel sits on a jungle-like cliff with plenty of monkeys. 4. The ride/adventure down to the beach. You have to try it to understand the highlight of our trip. 5. SPA was great. All in all, I would not stay at this hotel again, however I do recommend honeymooners spend at least two nights there.

title: Paradise yet somewhat lost.
review_excerpt: Well another trip another hotel. Sorry I forgot to take pictures. My wife and I decided to go to Bali for New Years. We were booking with AMEX points and the Bulgari Hotels & Resorts Bali was one of the hotels that would could book with points. The photos seemed amazing (and y...
review_text: Well another trip another hotel. Sorry I forgot to take pictures. My wife and I decided to go to Bali for New Years. We were booking with AMEX points and the Bulgari Hotels & Resorts Bali was one of the hotels that would could book with points. The photos seemed amazing (and yes the hotel, grounds, and rooms are stunning) so that is the hotel that we choose. Before arriving at the resort I emailed the hotel and they were very accommodating with arranging a pickup for our late arrival to the hotel. In fact we were greeted before we entered customs by a hotel staff member that walked us through the visa on arrival, walked us through around the customs line and had us processed by some higher up why we waited for our bags then got us to our car. On arrival we were taken directly to our room where they checked us in so that we did not even have to go to reception so late. Thank you. Below is my Pro's and Con's list. For every hotel I think you should always try and see both sides to everything so that people reading the reviews can make there own choice based on both sides. PROS: - The View. You can not go wrong anywhere in this hotel from the rooms to the restroom from reception to the pool. This is just fantastic. - The villa. We had a wonderful room king bed room. The bathroom was larger that the bedroom practically and the bedroom was not small at all. This was a small house basically. A wonderful outdoor shower as well as an indoor shower (please see the shower con below). The plunge pool was actually warm. We have stayed in hotels with plunge pools before. The problem is the hotels never heat them they rely on the sun. This one just perfect. Last but not least about the villa is the patio. It is wonderful and really big. There is no lack of space in these accommodations. - The staff. These are some of the nicest people out there. Good day and Roberto especially. These guys were so helpful and kind. - The food in the Indonesian restaurant was really good and the service was great. The italian restaurant that is supposed to be this great restaurant that has won awards was a let down. CONS: - The overall lack of attention to detail. I do not blame the staff at all. I think something like this is taught from the hotel management. My suggestion would be to steal some managers from the Four Seasons. - The shower. It seems like most people complain about this in the reviews. The floor is so rough that it feels like you are walking on the bottom of the sea with rocks on it. - Leakage. Yes with all of the rain our room was leaking from the ceilings and it looked like it had been happening for a while. In addition to this the moisture was coming through the walls and a great deal of this was around the electrical outlets. - Cleanliness. Since we checked in late we jumped into bed and went right to sleep. We woke up in the morning and the bed sheets looked like there was leftover room service stained on the sheets. Yuck. We took the sheets off and had the replaced. Three nights later it was the same thing again. They changed out the sheets and they were dirty again. This should never happen one time let only a second time. - The pool service. This is an example of lack of attention to detail. Once get to the pool you get the towel service and the water. After that other than a snack later you really never hear from anyone again not even if you want to order a drink. I would think a hotel would want to sell sell sell. The towels were pretty old and frayed. - Breakfast. This is a really odd one. When we checked in till about January 2nd the hotel was packed. The choices for breakfast were 4 things on the menu and some pastries, fruit and yogurt, and cereal in a buffet setting. It was not bad but what was really weird is that once the hotel emptied out we were greeted at breakfast and told that we could have anything we would like to have made for breakfast. This is not really a con it was just really weird. It seems like the hotel can not handle a large number of people (management). SUMMARY: This was a hard place to review actually. On one side it was just amazing and we walked out of the hotel loving our stay but we paid with points so the money is not a factor really. I think that if we had paid the huge sum of $1k per night we would have really been bummed out. There is a hotel that we went to for dinner called the Alila Uluwatu. This hotel is not the same style. It is stunning but very sleek and modern. It was very nice to walk around there at night and the food. WOW!!! it was awesome that is all that I can say. The staff were great as well. WOULD I STAY HERE AGAIN? Probably not. I would not be bummed if someone else paid for it but I would not pay for it. I would try out the Alila.

title: Extremely disappointing
review_excerpt: When I found out I was going to Bulgari Bali for my friends wedding, needless to say I was ecstatic. Unfortunately, the luxury branding of this resort is merely a name and the resort certainly fell very short of all it claimed to be. Why? 1. Difficult liaisons between administ...
review_text: When I found out I was going to Bulgari Bali for my friends wedding, needless to say I was ecstatic. Unfortunately, the luxury branding of this resort is merely a name and the resort certainly fell very short of all it claimed to be. Why? 1. Difficult liaisons between administrative staff with long waiting periods between emails. 2. I almost missed my flight when the lobby staff gave away my taxi to someone else even though I had been specifically told 5 mins earlier while sorting out my account that my taxi had arrived. There was no apology by the staff , and even a long argument that it was not my taxi. Finally, the lady who gave my taxi away liased with her colleague and realised that she had indeed made a mistake. I then had to wait 45 mins for another taxi and stressed about time. Traffic and the weather was bad taking more than an 1.5 hr to get to the airport . I arrived 50 mins before my flight ,and thankfully my airline still accepted me as 60 mins is usually the cut off time .Had I missed my flight I would have been very peeved as everything had been a disappointment from day 1. 3. My account was wrongly charged . 2 out of 3 rooms where overcharged- ensure you check the amount on your bill ( in usd) and your credit card if you are paying upfront. 4. Although I had booked for a consecutive night, the front staff still rang me twice to ask me when I was checking out. This was even after I had rang them to tell them to clean up the room and reminded them that I was staying another night. 5. Poorly maintained villas - we had two areas in our villa that was leaking . As December is a rainy period, our room was constantly leaking . The staff were good to put towels on the floor , but for a luxury hotel- this should definitely be attended to. 6. The breakfast alacarte menu was pretty dismal. The nasi goreng and mee goreng was tasty , but the Japanese breakfast had salmon that had gone bad . Again , another unacceptable occurrence . However , when we did request for mangosteens, the staff did th their best to accommodate us and whipped some up for us! 7. The beach was closed . Oh dear . Need I say more . My friends and I spent more time complaining about this resort than raving about it. However, in their defence , the butlers, staff driving the buggies, waiting staff, were all incredibly hospitable and friendly . I believe that they always tried their best to provide assistance wherever possible. The villas were well sized and tastefully furnished. Our welcome mochis were also delicious and a nice touch. Unfortunately , the admin and reservations staff did disappoint us greatly. The management should also look into putting more effort into maintaining their beautiful resort. I will probably not return to Bali Bulgari , as many have recommended other places such as Alila or St Regis , where you will have felt that your dollar was well worth it.

title: Beware of the monkeys here!
review_excerpt: We planned a trip here and thought it would be a dream vacation. It turned out to be a nightmare. This resort is beautiful, and is in an incredible spot. However, we did not realize it was inhabited by monkeys, as the resort is built on a cliffside of a former jungle that has ...
review_text: We planned a trip here and thought it would be a dream vacation. It turned out to be a nightmare. This resort is beautiful, and is in an incredible spot. However, we did not realize it was inhabited by monkeys, as the resort is built on a cliffside of a former jungle that has been home to the monkey population forever. We rented a cliffside villa, #11, for the stunning views. It was unfortunately the favorite hang out place for a large clan of monkeys (around 20). They invaded our space, opened the sliding doors, trashed the place and ate all the food (coffee packets included!) We had to keep calling for help to chase them off. It made me so uneasy, and we felt like prisoners in our own villa. If you don't lock the doors (all the rooms are separate spaces) they will come in. One brave rather large monkey followed us right into the bedroom to retrieve some fruit, and then proceeded to eat it on the doorstep! You cannot move freely about this type of villa without being invaded and feeling very vulnerable. The staff there are lovely people, but there is very little they can do other than assist you and say they are sorry about the monkeys. My complaint is that we had no warning from the hotel, except a little card on the bedside table alerting us to the "local inhabitants" and said don't leave your belongings outside, as the monkeys will take things! I had no idea it would be the most stressful trip of my life. These monkeys can be aggressive and have been known to bite people for no reason. There are other villas where this is not as much of a problem. But, if you are renting cliffside, you have been warned! These monkeys also enjoy invading the restaurant at breakfast time. It was comical to a degree. The staff are used to chasing them off. Glorious end to our stay was when the brakes failed on one of the buggies (golf cart) used to cart us up the steep roads to the entrance of the resort. We nearly missed being thrown over a wall because the driver hit a wall as we were hurling backwards. Nothing like a bit of whiplash and a few scrapes to end your 5 star vacation. I cannot recommend this place!

review_text: We were recently married at Bvlgari. If you would like details about our wedding, please send me a private message. This review is in regards to the accommodation only - we stayed in a 1 room villa and a 2 room villa. Positives: location is unbeatable with prime views of the Indian Ocean. Service is also generally attentive with prompt responses. Negatives: 1. The air conditioner in the 1 room villa did not work when we arrived (it was VERY HOT) and it took 2-3 hours for maintenance to fix. 2. Both toilets in the 2 room villa did not flush/were blocked throughout the day. This made the toilets pretty disgusting, especially as we had many people around on the wedding day. 3. The electricity did not work in the living area of the 2 room villa. Maintenance had to be called to fix. 4. Air conditioning in the living area of the 2 room villa was either faulty or broken. With all doors closed, you could still barely feel the air conditioning. 5. There was a Nespresso machine in the 2 room villa. However no pods were provided so the machine could not be used. 6. The keys to the villas were difficult to use and constantly jammed in the doors. Unfortunately all the negatives ruined our stay. Ultimately at this price range, you are paying for premium service and the finer details to be perfect. While I believe the service is quite good, the finer details definitely leave alot to be desired and the resort as a whole is in desperate need of better maintenance. I would not spend the money on this resort again until they improve their maintenance as for the amount of money, you could go to many other better places in the world.

review_excerpt: Awesome location/view and the best pool ever. HOWEVER, extremely outdated villas, which is just a complete shame given the level of luxury associated with bulgari resorts. I'm not talking about major face lift here but small stuff that could make a huge difference. Falling doo...
review_text: Awesome location/view and the best pool ever. HOWEVER, extremely outdated villas, which is just a complete shame given the level of luxury associated with bulgari resorts. I'm not talking about major face lift here but small stuff that could make a huge difference. Falling door knobs, ever-clogging sinks, worn down chairs and tables, hideous lamps and lighting, phones from the 80's. for the money they charge, definitely under-invested. Also, seems like they don't do anything in containing flies and Mosquitos. Every meal we had, we were sharing our food with tens of flies that often end up swimming in our drinks. FYI, ended up moving to another resort nearby, and we had no fly/mosquito issues there. Staffs. Friendly yet not professional. For luxury resorts, smiles don't and shouldn't cut it. When we checked in, we were escorted to our villa but no explalation as to facility locations, etc, which is at, all times, provided at othetluxury resorts. We weren't even given the resort map... Beach. Was closed during our stay. We were aware but would expect the resort to keep the guests posted on when it would reopen. We had to ask everyday and was provided different answers/dates on a daily basis. Very annoying. Foods. Overall, very good but breakfast needs some revamping. Very limited selections vs other resorts we've been to. And pls contain those flies!! Pretty sick to see flies swarming over anything and everything laid on the buffet tables! In sum, one of the most beautiful resorts we've been to only to be completely tarnished/offset by hideous villas that are significantly underinvested. We couldn't stand it and moved to another resort nearby. For the management, would seriously recommend you to take a good look through the villas and invest accordingly / at par with the level of luxury/comfort the bulgari name commands or should command.

review_text: For the price you pay to stay at the Bulgari I think there are much better options in Bali, such as Alila, St Regis and The Four Seasons. For this level of hotel I expected much more. Aesthetically the resort was absolutely gorgeous and the room was beautiful. The fit out was of a very high quality as you would expect. Here is where i was let down: Upon arrival we had to wait 30 minutes to get into our room, we arrived after the check in time and i would have expected the room to be ready right away. The pool in the villa was cloudy , has dead worms in it and was dirty, In the evening we arrived back from dinner to find our room swarming with flying bugs, we called and had a man arrive, followed by another 3 staff who sprayed the bugs and informed me we had a nest of them at out front door, it was at this point that i would have expected a room transfer. Instead the room was left for us with all of the dead bugs on the floor and not cleaned up. We continues to have them invade our bed throughout the night which made for an uncomfortable and unromantic night. The hotel cannot control the bugs but they could have moved us to another room with no nest or at least cleaned up the dead bugs. The next morning we went to breakfast that was underwhelming, the eggs were cold and the yoghurt was warm, the service however was excellent, Upon return from breakfast i would have expected the bugs to at least be cleaned from our room but they were not. What I did like was the beautiful resort itself, the service from the butler and the restaurant staff, the bed was absolutely amazing, the most comfortable bed, doona and pillows i have ever slept in, the view was lovely. All in all I would not go again instead i would go to the Four Seasons which is at a better location, has better service and food or the St Regis where I could also say the same.

review_text: Beautiful resort, but not private by any means. Tourist buses aplenty here, but because of the spread out nature of the resort, you can isolate yourself a bit more in your own room. The rooms were sadly a bit dated, as I think a lot of our floors might’ve been rotting. The bathroom was awesome and visually stunning, complete with Bulgari style designs in small accents throughout the room. We stayed in a cliffs edge view villa, and the view was magnificent. The service was a bit spotty; you can see where they try to be on top of it, but at the end of the day, there are just too many people and it doesn’t feel very personal. However, when they can, they will try to personalize for you. I was feeling under the weather and the front desk woman at the restaurant sent me some local remedies to our room just in case, and remembered to check in with me the next morning. The resort is amazing, but I think the private room pool views were even better. Also, its totally true what they say about the monkeys, holy crap they’re scary.

review_excerpt: We stayed at Bulgari during the Easter long weekend. We were glad that we used their airport transfer service as it is our first time to Bali and didn't know our way around. Bulgari staffs took care all of these for us. During our stay there, Bulgari staffs tried their best to...
review_text: We stayed at Bulgari during the Easter long weekend. We were glad that we used their airport transfer service as it is our first time to Bali and didn't know our way around. Bulgari staffs took care all of these for us. During our stay there, Bulgari staffs tried their best to make sure that we are happy and satisfied. One thing to note that is the buggy did take about 10 minutes to come. Maybe Bulgari should have a few more buggies to minimise the waiting time. We were running late for a few appointments because of this. I also really enjoyed the food served by both italian and Sangkar restaurants as well as in room dining. We ate at the Italian restaurant and were given complimentary breads, starter, sorbet ( to clean our palate) and chocolate truffles and turkish delight. Food was amazing and yummylicious. However, there was a monkey inside our villa on our last night in Bulgari and making such a loud noise ( right above our bed) that we couldnt sleep. We were initally told by the staffs to go back to sleep and they tried to convince us its a crab. In the end, we moved to another villla at 4.30am. Our plan to watch the sunrise and photographtaking was ditched as we were tired after this monkey drama. We stayed at villa 57, not close to the cliff at all. We had same issue that some of the reviewers had - the door is quite hard to open! We also had one of our best spa experiences here. I would conclude that my overall stay at Bulgari Bali was lovely but there is room for improvement. I will come back again providing that there is no monkey invading my villa.

title: spectacular setting, but is it in Bali??
review_excerpt: The Bulgari is very well designed by the Citterio Office, it is made in the euro-asian (hip)syle: black and dark woods and stones, finest decor and linen in the villas, Bulgari amenities. The setting is extremely spectacular, the elevator to the beach site and the beach are un...
review_text: The Bulgari is very well designed by the Citterio Office, it is made in the euro-asian (hip)syle: black and dark woods and stones, finest decor and linen in the villas, Bulgari amenities. The setting is extremely spectacular, the elevator to the beach site and the beach are unique, truely incredible (no swimming due to waves, but doesnt matter)! But: there is not a single sign or hint, showing you being in Bali!Nothing typical, everywhere just anonymous design. It could have been expected, that a Balinese resort refers more to traditional signs, esp. when made by a worldwide renowned design-studio. Only 2 restaurants (Italian and Asian); minus: who (except some Japanese or Taiwanese) wants to eat Italian food in Bali?? Asian food is a bit boring, menu doesnt change. So food and wines were not a highlight like all resorts we been in Bali the weeks before. The "butler-system" is more on a housekeeping-basis, and not really working. Staff is OK, but not too friendly. In our impression, they are trained to be a little formal, not so warm, helpful and frinedly like all other Balines we got to know during our trip. Overall it was a very pleasant 3-days cool-down of our Bali-trip, but nothing to remind long. The gorgeous views and the beach elevator are worth the stay, but dont miss to stay elsewhere in the country (esp. at The Chedi Club in Ubud)!

title: 5 stars hardware 2 stars services
review_excerpt: I stayed in Bulgari Resorts in September 2010, as I was impressed by this location and it's branding and expecting the level of services they could offer. However, I find out that the way they manage the hotel is more on corporate standard rather than personal touch. Butler se...
review_text: I stayed in Bulgari Resorts in September 2010, as I was impressed by this location and it's branding and expecting the level of services they could offer. However, I find out that the way they manage the hotel is more on corporate standard rather than personal touch. Butler services is not personal, I didn't get the name of the Butler who drove me to my villa, he just asked to me to press 1 for any assistance, then pressing 1 on the phone actually connected you to the call center and then they despatch a *butler* or staff to help you. They introduced all in-villa faciliates to me during the check-in, I was impressed by the free wi-fi access and trying to connect the Internet when I settled down. It didn't work, I then press 1 to call the Bulter and then they connected me to the helpdesk, they then send *IT Support* or Techican to my villa to fix it, eventually I found out the staff do not know anything about how it works, I eventually rolled up my sleeve and fixed it myself in front of the staff they despatched. I also found out the helpdesk is in Manila. They spent 40mins on it. What a globalized way of managing a 6 stars luxious resort. Location-wise, Uluwatu is kind of remote than any part of Bali, it offers you privacy but also costs some time to travel when dining out. I'd give credit to all the in-room hardware, B&O TV sets, washroom setup, your own private garden in the villa for your workout, cliff side endless swimming pool overlooking Indian Ocean. However, all these setting must first come with the highly professional services and personal touch. With the room rate I was paying, I could enjoy similar level of environment and excellent customer services by COMO Hotel group. I can still name a few bad experience of how poor the bulter services e.g. missing the promise of pickup....at the end I found out the ratio of Bulter to Villa to 1 to 10, given there are 50 villa or more. That was in 2010. I hope they improve.

mismatch_types: low overall rating with high aspect rating
title: Overpriced, Nothing Special
review_excerpt: My fiance and I stayed for 2 nights at the Bulgari before we moved to the Alila Villas for 3 nights (also see my review on Alilia) as I couldn't decide which one to go for so I decided to check out both. Turned out Alilia was far better than expected and Bulgari is a bit disap...
review_text: My fiance and I stayed for 2 nights at the Bulgari before we moved to the Alila Villas for 3 nights (also see my review on Alilia) as I couldn't decide which one to go for so I decided to check out both. Turned out Alilia was far better than expected and Bulgari is a bit disappointed. It is the most expensive in terms of the room rate and the food is just ridicously overpriced. A bowl of noodle soup is 40 usd and it doesn't even taste that good. I have been to some of the best resorts in South Africa and I was expecting with a brand like Bulgari, I could expect it to be at the same level but it clearly was not. It is similar to say a Four Season in Koh Samui but much more expensive, even the food tasted much better at a Four Season. The service is good and not much surprises. We had breakfast in our villa the first morning and got surrounded by 20 monkeys trying to steal our food, we called for help to clear out the food however it took them another 15-20mins to come over, so my fiance and I had to grab whatever tea or fodd that we wanted to go back into the room watching the monkeys took over our table outside and eat all the leftovers. It was an interesting experince with nature i suppose but help could have come a bit quicker. The room itself is nicer and big however again not much different than any high end tropical resorts and the design doesn't have any "Bulgari" feel. It's not posh or modern, just typical Balinese dark wood style. The Alila looks more Bulgari than the Bulgari...! They do have B&O TV however a Philips alarm clock? and no Nepresso machine although it's an italian hotel?!! The villa pool is also tiny, although it was funny watching the monkeys dipping into it. The main pool is nice with gorgeous ocean view but again not so big. Food selection is so limited and overpriced, and worst doesn't taste so good. We even tried the Mozerralla pizza for a snack and it was bad, come on, this is again an Italian resort right? The food is 3 times as expensive as a normal resort just because the Nasi Goreng comes with Lobster tail not chicken...but I don't like lobster! Nicest thing is probably that they have a lift to take you down to the beach (so save wathcing the thousand steps) and they also have a bar and lounge chairs there so it was nice to check out the beach although there were more bugs and the waves were fierce. The Spa was good, similar to the one at Alila, they use Espa from the UK and Bulgari amenities which are better than the Alila brand, but again treatments are rather expensive. Given there are so many other new choices in Uluwatu and I love the Alila so much more, I definitely won't go back to Bulgari.

mismatch_types: low overall rating with high aspect rating
title: Beautiful views.. but bugs are a real issue.
review_excerpt: I'll start with the good.... The Property is beautiful... breathtaking, as are the villas. It truly is memorable and unique and that's really why this hotel is so highly rated and so over priced. Food was pretty as were the restaurants, no complaints on the service. Now the ba...
review_text: I'll start with the good.... The Property is beautiful... breathtaking, as are the villas. It truly is memorable and unique and that's really why this hotel is so highly rated and so over priced. Food was pretty as were the restaurants, no complaints on the service. Now the bad... The hotel is in a remote part of Bali on the southern tip making it out of the way, but obviously that's a logistical reality of having such a breathtaking view from the entire hotel. However, being in such a remote place with the set up of the entirely outside villa hotel has it's challenges, and the management clearly has not figured it out, and for that, you should stay away. The biggest problem: mosquitoes. I have left and am now at the St. Regis. I just counted, I have 17 bites, noticeable ones that itch. Arms, Legs, Ankles, Forehead, Back of Neck, etc. Before coming to Bali I was very aware of mosquitoes, I wore repellant the entire time I was here, including to sleep at night in the villa since it was obvious there were mosquitoes INSIDE our villa (#34). It was miserable, it felt like you couldn't get away from them. I was both happy and sad to leave, but more happy. When you pay $700+ per night to a hotel, you shouldn't leave with bug bites. The management of this hotel needs to seriously consider what it can do. Every AM their breakfast brunch is great, except i am a big fan of Muesli and both times I had the brunch there were ants in the Muesli. Another representative issue of how the bugs are a real problem here and management hasn't figure it out yet. To contrast I am at the St. Regis. It's not as breathtaking but is still beautiful, has better access to the beach and is considerably cheaper. The room is equally as nice / beautiful, just smaller, but still incredibly spacious. Most important, there are no mosquitoes and now I am applying calamine lotion trying not to let the bugs not bother the rest of my Asian trip. Really, this hotel should refund me for jeopardizing my entire trip. Fix your mosquito problem. Stick the torches up that keep the bugs away. Maybe I am oversimplifying the issue, but you have to do something about the bugs. I may post pictures later of all my mosquito bites but I figure I'll spare the TravelAdvisor community and you'll take my word for it.

review_text: We stayed at villa 29 for two nights, pretty good view to the ocean, right beside the reception building. very clean room, and the bathroom is super big with a bathtub, outdoor and indoor shower rooms. a little hand written welcome card was placed on our desk, very personal and delightful. the staffs were all friendly, helpful and polite, they greeted us by our names. buggy always came fast to pick us up anytime. our butler (Mr Ode) was very hands on to our needs. wifi connection covered almost the whole villa area, fast, free, and reliable too. if i can mention the downsides were: - doors in the villa are hard to open or lock (maybe i just didn't get the trick to do it) - the lighting in the bedroom is too dark for me - mosquitoes buzzing in my ear for the two nights we stayed there, even after applying the mosquito repellant - the sound of transporting buggies was heard pretty loudly from our room even after we closed all doors - the breakfast food taste and varieties are so so. would expect more for such a big name Bulgari - room air conditioning suddenly not cold enough at night (happened twice) one more thing; on the second morning at the breakfast place, my wife was approaching to the empty table at the corner of the patio. but she was stopped by a waiter telling her that the table had been taken (there were no reserved signs on it). so we took the table right behind it. and after the 35 mins+ we were there, nobody came and the table remained empty even after the breakfast time ended. we were just wondering why we weren't allowed to sit there. anyway, it's a very good resort if you want to stay in and enjoy the quite and be in touch with the more natural side of Bali

mismatch_types: low overall rating with high aspect rating
title: Less for More
review_excerpt: On paper, this seems to be the perfect romantic getaway...a landscape touched by gods, a world-class luxury brand / hotel management company and bricks-&-mortars designed by Antonio Citterio. Bulgari, Bali, almost could have been the perfect resort...except for one "minor" fau...
review_text: On paper, this seems to be the perfect romantic getaway...a landscape touched by gods, a world-class luxury brand / hotel management company and bricks-&-mortars designed by Antonio Citterio. Bulgari, Bali, almost could have been the perfect resort...except for one "minor" fault...service. While the service is not offensive, it lacks the personable and customized ambience that other top-notch resorts offer. You get about the same amount of attention as if you are wearing shorts and sneakers browsing through a Bulgari jewelry store. If you pay this amount of money for a stay, you expect much more. During my stay, I filed a minor complaint on housekeeping. I repeatedly (3x) asked for a response...if not an apology. None was offered. Since this resort is blessed in so many ways, it definitely attracts the wedding crowd. If you enjoy privacy, then cross your fingers on not meeting the wedding entourage. The packaged weddings seem to be a big draw here. It's great for the newlyweds and the wedding party; not so great if you are on your special romantic getaway. I do have compliments for this resort. The cliff-side view is breath-taking. The 75 degree tram down to the beach was spectacular. The Italian restaurant, Il Ristorante, was fantastic (BTW...if you want Indonesian food, try Warung at Alila not far away). The villas in a typical Balinese garden setting were beautiful...though the dark furnitures in a humid tropic weather do not age well. I might sound overly critical. However, I am entitled at the rates they charge. There are simply many other resorts on this beautiful island which offer more for less.

mismatch_types: low overall rating with high aspect rating
title: Stunning setting let down by inadequate service
review_excerpt: For such an expensive resort, it is only fair to judge it on a strict scale and for that, Bvlgari in Bali is only Average. The view is stunning and the amenities are top notch, but the service and other small details that usually separate a good hotel from a great one were all...
review_text: For such an expensive resort, it is only fair to judge it on a strict scale and for that, Bvlgari in Bali is only Average. The view is stunning and the amenities are top notch, but the service and other small details that usually separate a good hotel from a great one were all lacking during my stay. Room: The room is nice. Having stayed at Banyan Tree in Ungasan, I would prefer BT's whole set up and pool. However, Bvlgari amenities and B&O equipment are hard to beat. The rooms are quite clean. The let down for me would be the patio area and the pool. It's just on the small side. The villa was aging and you can tell from the damage on the wooden doors and walls. The bed is really soft and for those who prefer harder mattresses, you may find this slightly uncomfortable. Facilities: My favorite part of this resort is the main pool. The view is stunning and the two mini inifinty pools off to the side are great for couples and friends to share. And where else can you take a shower outdoors while enjoying unobstructed views of the ocean? The gym was not that big, but also not a huge concern for me. Breakfast: The spread is small. You get the bulk of your food from the ala carte menu. The quality is not that great. Again, considering the brand and the price that you pay at this resort, it's fair to expect something great. Unfortunately, I was quite disappointed. Service at breakfast was also slow. It needs to be more proactive. I understand that things tend to be slower in Bali as it should be, but slow doesn't mean inefficient. Service: Overall service at this property is ok if this were a regular 4 or 5 star property. As one of the top hotels in Bali, I expected something mind-blowing. At Banyan Tree, almost everyone remembers your name whether it's the buggy drivers, waiters/waitresses, or the pool attendants. At Bvlgari, you still get kind and genuine service, but it doesn't feel as personal. And again, not always that efficient. Some staff were wonderful, but the consistency just wasn't there. Check-in/Check-out: No major concerns. Fast and no problems with the bill. Would I return? Maybe. It depends. Overall, I enjoyed my time at Bvlgari. You just can't beat that view. It would be nice if they improved on the service and food.

mismatch_types: high aspect rating with negative text sentiment
title: the price of peace in Bali is - priceless
review_excerpt: I organized an incentive group of high level business people, 115 people in all, where we took over residency of the whole of Bulgari Resort in late May 2011. These days, it appears that usually people are very quick to write something negative on TripAdvisor in the hope if re...
review_text: I organized an incentive group of high level business people, 115 people in all, where we took over residency of the whole of Bulgari Resort in late May 2011. These days, it appears that usually people are very quick to write something negative on TripAdvisor in the hope if receiving recompense, an upgrade or "forcing the hotelier under fear of a bad review" to give in to unreasonable demands, so I felt compelled to write about our experience. The logistics of planning, managing, housing, feeding and watering such a large group for 5 days, along with entertainment, internal and external cannot be underestimated. With infinite and detailed planning, and meetings as detailed as I have ever encountered, I can say the teams and staff at Bulgari did not let us down in any way, either by minor errors, or because of unplanned events, or due to lack of attentiveness, process or product. The old adage, "you get what you pay for" could not have been more true. We paid top dollar, and we got exceptional experiences, service and an unforgettable event - that happily, was also enjoyable to manage. F&B, housekeeping, maintenance, senior management all contributed to the success, and the enjoyment by all. in tropical climates you will always find bugs, live with it or stay at home. Wear and tear by relentless weather and water of these climates, combined with the salt corrosion and damage from being literally at the shoreline (just ask any boatie) is a constant maintenance nightmare and can show major signs of wear and tear overnight. Being so close to nature you have the wonder of wild animals, not the wild animals you encounter in Kuta and who lets face it are infinitely more dangerous, an unspoilt beach means just that - no plastic bottles, no plastic bags, no straws, preserved naturally just as mother nature presented it in the first place. Wooden tables carved from locally sourced trees, and stunning displays of craftsmanship in heavy Indonesian teak chairs were on offer, if people took the time to recognize this from a modern cushioned plastic chair. The Resort does show signs of having being used in places, it is not new, but it also oozes peace, tranquility and understated elegance. discreet service is just that - there when you need it - not constantly being pestered Being able to retreat from the madness of other parts of today's Bali was for our group, priceless.

review_text: I just came back from Bvglari , Uluwatu and had a lovely last evening but there were a number of hiccups and this is not to blame entirely on the management, but there was a lack of consistency in SOP with the staff. Some things can be let go of, but for the price you pay, I know having been to smaller resorts in Bali, you expect Value Exchange. 1.The room was beautiful and clean, but 2/3 times with the Do Not Disturb sign on, our room was entered anyway. 2. When I asked staff for a missing room coffee filter for the loose Coffee to strain, the staff had no idea and neither attempted to find out and get back to me. 3. We ordered the golf karts to the lobby , and 2/3 times they were VERY late and we were nearly late for the wedding in the estate across from us. I felt they should have let us know a time frame, so perhaps we could have walked to the lobby instead. It took 30-45mins. 4. Ordering items like cigarettes took just as long, 30 misn and we had to call at least 3 times. 5. At breakfast I was refused in the buffet area with a bikini top and shorts, which I understood, so we were told to sit at the bar just below, which in fact was a lot quieter and a lot more intimate, which we really appreciated. The next day we asked to sit there again, dressed appropriately this time, but different staff, said different things with allowing us and we were left hanging a bit too long, so with lack of consistency in training, they should be able to tell us off the cuff what we can or cannot do. We ended up eating there any way. 6. I had a mini ordeal with my mobile dropping into the pool, so I asked if it was okay to have a tiny bowl of uncooked rice, which we honestly thought after all the waiting we would not expect to be charged , so we were surprised when they wanted to charge us for the price of cooked rice. 7. The last day at dinner , my partner who enjoys drinking Coke cola ordered a glass and upon drinking it , he let the staff know this was diet coke or either Pepsi, which he does not like . I tried it and let the staff know they made a mistake, fair enough. But they came back and insisted it was coke. We ordered a bottle of still water on the side of our meal , and they filled our glasses once and we never saw the other half of the bottle. When the bill came, the order said "Diet Coke", and it was obvious they HAD made a mistake and charged us for a full bottle of still which we would have drank if it was left on our table. We had voiced our concerns to the Director of Rooms, Marcel, who was a fantastic guy who understood our frustrations at the small things, but to be honest we had only wanted the name of the MOD when we asked the bar staff and they ended up calling the BAr manager who then called the D.O.R and we really wanted to eat our breakfast in peace, but they ran around calling all the managers when we JUST wanted a name. We explained none of them were in trouble we just wanted a Name. 8. Lastly consistency in breakfast was average. The first day we had a lovely Eggs Benedict on bread with potato , fried tomato, with chicken sausage and bacon, the next day, there was no sausage or no bacon with the meal?!? Well overall , I still would like to go back, I just wish they would take care of the smaller details for the price that you pay.

review_text: Lacks a unique edge that its above-premium price, Bulgari branding and the reputation of its Milan sister-property (where I've stayed) all demand. Not a bad resort but there are 20 other better and/or better priced alternatives in Bali and nearby. Villas are spacious and well-appointed, although the private plunge pools are all overlooked by other villas. Resort is well laid out but could ANO 5+ star spa; makes little use of the only unique feature: a private cable car to the beach. But since the beach bar shuts at 5pm, there's little point venturing down for a romantic sundowner. Service is friendly and polite, but (again) lacks that premium edge you'd expect and could be any other pleasant similar resort in SE Asia. Probably the biggest disappointment is the F&B - given the distance from any alternative dining venues, I expected more. What you get is an average 'International' restaurant, that served some of the poorest Asian food I've had in the region, and an Italian restaurant where the mains average US$70+ -- acceptable perhaps if you have a Michelin Star, or even if you're serving amazing food, but for tiny portions of same-same pasta, I felt cheated. Overall a pleasant place, but very overpriced and trading off a brand that it doesn't deserve.
